requiredBehaviors.behavior: AgenC tracks a current turn with bounded recent-turn history, monotonic turn ids, message records, tool calls, per-turn state, and donor-compatible stats.
Live callers use isolated MultiTurnContextManager instances so histories, state maps, options, and compaction thresholds cannot leak across daemon sessions.
Per-turn token accounting combines message and attachment tokens, compares them to max-turn and automatic compaction thresholds, and exposes a stable should-compact status.

The manager records explicit AGENC.md, session-memory, and file-mention attachments by wrapping already-landed AgenC helpers, preserving rejection/warning metadata and counting attachment tokens once.

File mentions require an explicit cwd, honor allowed roots, reject outside-root and symlink escapes through the wrapper, and propagate unexpected helper failures without dropping previously recorded attachments.

intentionalReductions: RT-02 does not wire the live turn loop; downstream RT and PR items consume the per-session manager factory.
The module-level donor API is retained as a source-parity compatibility surface only. Production callers should use createMultiTurnContextManager.
The manager wraps existing AGENC.md, session-memory, file-mention, token-estimation, and compaction helpers instead of re-porting those subsystems.
